characters.name: "Mom" and "Pop"
characters.description: Mom and Pop run a shoe repair store in Jerry's neighborhood. Kramer trys to drum up business for them inThe Mom and Pop Storeand they are played byElsa RavenandMichael Robello. Mom and Pop are a childless couple, making their handles "Mom" and "Pop" fraudulent. Kramer's well meaning suggestion to Mom and Pop to have their store's electrical wiring be looked at drives them out of business. Thanks to Kramer, they were in possession of Jerry's vast sneaker collection at the time they closed up shop. Jerry confronts them after learning they are selling his sneakers at a New Jersey garage sale.

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: AaronGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe Raincoats, Part 1Last appearanceThe Raincoats, Part 2Portrayed byJudge Reinhold[Source] Aaronis a minor character on the television showSeinfeld. Aaron datedElainein “The Raincoats”, and he is played byJudge Reinhold. Aaron is a close-talker, a highly quirky way of speaking to people. He will walk up to the person he is engaging in conversation as closely as possible with his face mere inches away from theirs.

characters.name: Tony Abado
characters.description: Tony AbadoGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe Bottle DepositOccupationMechanicPortrayed byBrad Garrett[Source] Tony Abadois a highly skilled auto mechanic who grew deeply attached toJerry Seinfeld's Saab convertible. He was played byBrad Garrettand appeared in Season 7's hour-long episode "The Bottle Deposit". Tony is an easygoing guy if the customer treats his car right. If he doesn't, he will guilt-trip him with warnings of the dangers of poor automobile maintenance. He has a program for neglectful car owners to bring their cars back to showroom standards, but it involves a commitment to keeping theirs car below 60 mph at all times, making sure their ratio of left turns is equal to turns to the right and the spending of possibly thousands of dollars. Anyone who objects to this program is brow-beaten with even more scorn and ridicule from him.

characters.name: Mickey Abbott
characters.description: Mickey AbbottGenderMaleFamilyFather:Dr. AbbottMother: Mrs. AbbottUnnamed Ex-WifeWife:Karen AbbottUnnamed kidsFirst appearanceThe Stand InLast appearanceThe Finale, Part 2OccupationActorPortrayed byDanny Woodburn[Source] Mickey Abbottis the “little person” friend ofCosmo Kramer. Mickey and Kramer met while acting as stand-ins for a soap opera. Kramer was the lead's stand-in while Mickey was the stand-in for his son. They also were co-workers at a department store where Kramer was Santa while Mickey was an elf. Mickey dislikes the term "midget".

characters.name: Ada
characters.description: AdaGenderFemaleFirst appearanceThe SecretaryLast appearanceThe RaceOccupationSecretaryPortrayed byVicki Lewis[Source] AdawasGeorge Costanza’s secretary with theNew York Yankees. She was portrayed byVicki Lewis. In "The Secretary", George didn't want to hire an attractive secretary so he could focus on his work, and in the interview process turned down a few women specifically because they were so attractive. George ultimately hired Ada, whose efficiency impressed him. One day while they are working together, George becomes attracted to Ada, and the two have sex. While having sex George blurts out that he's giving Ada a raise.Jerrysuggests George undo his promise the next time they have sex, but George sees no other option than to go toSteinbrennerand demand that Ada be given a raise, even though she has only been with the franchise for 3 days. Ada is given a raise, but it turns out that her new wages are even greater than George's, much to his annoyance.

characters.name: Adam (The Ugly Baby)
characters.description: Adam (The Ugly Baby)GenderMaleFamilyFather:MichaelMother:CarolSister:Unnamed SisterFirst appearanceThe Hamptons, mentionedLast appearanceThe Soul Mate, mentioned[Source] Adamwas originally known as the ugly baby ofCarolandMichaelwhen he was introduced in "The Hamptons". Later, in the "The Soul Mate", Carol offers to show Elaine pictures of him and reveals that his name is Adam. While it is implied that Adam had grown since Elaine last saw him, he still appears to be ugly as the sight of the pictures causes Elaine to turn away in obvious disgust.

characters.name: Agent Stone
characters.description: Agent StoneGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe Baby ShowerOccupationFBI AgentPortrayed byGeorge C. Simms[Source] Agent Stoneis an FBI agentJerrydreamt about on a plane. Jerry dreamt that the FBI discovered he had installed illegal cable in his apartment. In the dream, he tried to convince them that who they really wanted was the Russian guy who installed the cable. The Russian turned out to be Agent Stone undercover. Trying to escape, they shot at Jerry. While on the floor, Agent Stone shot Jerry, killing him. Jerry then woke up, scared, and had to stop the illegal hook-up. He is portrayed byGeorge C. Simms.

characters.name: Alan
characters.description: AlanGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe Andrea DoriaPortrayed byTom Gallop[Source] Alanwas set up on a date withElaine Benesby a mutual friend. He was played byTom Gallopand appears in "The Andrea Doria". Elaine likes Alan, but ends the relationship when she finds out he is a 'bad breaker-upper', feeling breaking up well is one of the most important parts of a relationship. When Alan ends a relationship, he hones in on something awkward about his ex-girlfriend and makes fun of her about it. Some of his former lovers are so racked with anger over Alan's painful jibes that they physically attack him if they run into him later on. When breaking up with Elaine, Alan calls her 'big-head', and claims her head is too big for her body. Elaine laughs it off at first, but grows very self-conscious about Alan's insult as time passes. To get over it, Elaine meets up with Alan to show him she wasn't affected by his comments, but winds up attacking him when he tells her she not only has a very large head, but also a noticeable bump in her nose.

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: AmandaGenderFemaleFirst appearanceThe Bizarro JerryOccupationReceptionistPortrayed byJustina Vail[Source]Amandawas one ofGeorge Costanza's girlfriends, who worked as a receptionist at Brandt/Leland. She appeared only once, in “The Bizarro Jerry”. George gets a date with her by showing her a photo ofGillian, passing her off as his late fiancéeSusan Ross. After she takes him to a night club frequented by her model friends, he becomes more interested in them and breaks up with her.

characters.name: Amy
characters.description: AmyGenderFemaleFirst appearanceThe GlassesPortrayed byAnna Gunn[Source] Amyis a woman whomJerrydated in "The Glasses"; she was portrayed byAnna Gunn. Everything was going well between Jerry and Amy until she saw another side to him, an ugly side.Georgethought he saw her making out with Jerry's cousinJeffrey, even though he wasn't wearing his glasses (he was squinting). Jerry has a plan to find out if they did make out, bring her with him when he goes to Jeffrey's apartment and if they did in fact make out there would be no hiding it between them. When Amy and Jerry arrive at Jeffrey's, they find thatUncle Leois there instead which pretty much derails Jerry's plan. Jerry mistakenly thinks that Jeffrey has confessed to making out with Amy when Uncle Leo gives him a message that says he's very sorry, and he hopes he can forgive him. It turns out that Amy has never met Jeffrey and he was only apologizing because the seats at the concert were not very good. This misunderstanding led to their breakup.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: AngelaGenderFemaleFirst appearanceThe Good SamaritanPortrayed byMelinda McGraw[Source] Angelais the hit-and-run driver in “The Good Samaritan”. She datedJerry Seinfeld. She was played byMelinda McGraw. Angela essentially threatened to kill Jerry if he reported her to the police for hitting the victim and leaving the scene.

characters.name: Antonio
characters.description: AntonioGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe BusboyOccupationBusboyPortrayed byDavid Labiosa[Source] Antoniois a busboy whomGeorgegot fired from his job in "The Busboy"; Antonio was played byDavid Labiosa. One evening while George,JerryandElaineare out to dinner, they notice a menu has caught on fire. They rush to put it out and George tells the manager that the busboy might have put the menu too close to a candle that was on the table, with Elaine jokingly stating that she'll never eat there again. The busboy, Antonio, gets chewed out by his boss and is fired.

characters.name: Arnold Deensfrei
characters.description: Arnold Deensfreiis a minor character in Seinfeld. He is played byRobert Katims. Arnold first appears in "The Jimmy" as an executive if AMCA (short for Able Mentally Challenged Adults). He thought Kramer was mentally challenged when he met him in a taxi cab. He appeared in "The Finale, Part 2" attending the trial inLatham County. A deleted scene had him mentioning to District AttorneyHoytabout how Kramer pretended to be mentally challenged. Kramer stated that it was the novacane that made him do that as JudgeArthur Vandelaytells Kramer to be quiet.

characters.name: Artie Levine
characters.description: Artie LevineGenderMaleFamilyCousin:Jerry SeinfeldUncle:Morty SeinfeldAunt:Helen SeinfeldFirst appearanceThe Stake OutPortrayed byRon Steelman[Source] Artie LevineisJerry'scousin. He drives a "wagon" and insists on pronouncing his last name "La-Vine" as opposed to "La-Veen". He was portrayed byRon Steelman.

characters.description: Artie LevineGenderMaleFamilyCousin:Jerry SeinfeldUncle:Morty SeinfeldAunt:Helen SeinfeldFirst appearanceThe Stake OutPortrayed byRon Steelman[Source] Artie LevineisJerry'scousin. characters.name: Henry Atkins
characters.description: Henry AtkinsGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe Junk MailOccupationPostmaster GeneralPortrayed byWilford Brimley[Source]United States Postmaster General Henry Atkinsis the sitting Postmaster General in “The Junk Mail”. He is an elderly gentleman, and he appears to have a short temper. He is also very intimidating, and is presumably ruthless. He was portrayed by the lateWilford Brimley. In “The Junk Mail”,Kramergets upset with how many Pottery Barn catalogues he constantly gets. He then goes to the post office and demands to stop getting mail. They refuse, so he carries around a dummy, dressed in a mailman uniform, with a bucket over its head.Newmanbegins to warn Kramer the dangers of doing this, but they are both captured. Kramer is taken to an interrogation room where he is met by Postmaster General Henry Atkins. Henry informs Kramer that he came all the way up from Washington, D.C., just to see him and missed a game of golf with the Secretary of State. He takes extreme exception to Kramer and other people who take him away from his golf, and he angrily lets Kramer know it! However, he finally convinces Kramer to keep his mail service. As Kramer leaves, Newman is hauled into the room, wearing handcuffs and a bucket over his head. Newman tells Kramer, "Tell the world my story." Kramer leaves, but Newman's punishment awaits. Wilford Brimley's portrayal of Henry Atkins is a parody of his role as an Assistant Attorney General inAbsence of Malice.

characters.name: Kenny Bania
characters.description: Kenny BaniaKenny BaniaGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe SoupLast appearanceThe FinaleOccupationStand-up ComedianPortrayed bySteve Hytner[Source] Kenny Baniais a fellow comedian with whomJerryfrequently comes into contact on the New York City comedy circuit. He is a "hack" standup comedian, much loathed by Jerry. Bania is portrayed bySteve Hytner. Bania looks up to Jerry and frequently tries to impress him with recommendations to miscellaneous services and products. He follows up his suggestions by saying, "It's the best, Jerry! The best!" Conversely, Jerry intensely dislikes Bania for both his exuberance and his perceived lack of talent.

characters.name: Barry
characters.description: "Mr. Kramer, he's an innocent primate." "So am I." Barry is a chimpanzee who lives at the New York Zoo and appears inThe Face Painter. While waiting to get a personal tour of the zoo from George's girlfriend Siena,Cosmo Krameris struck in the face by a banana peel thrown by Barry. Kramer retaliates by pelting the monkey in the back of the head with the same banana peel. Both Kramer and Barry walk away from the experience with hurt feelings.

The character has a close physical resemblance to George. Michael has a panic attack right before the filming of the pilot and he confides toJerrythat he has forgotten all of his lines.

characters.description: Not to be confused withBen Cantwell.BenGenderMaleFirst appearanceThe AbstinenceOccupationDoctorPortrayed byBob Odenkirk[Source] Benis a doctor who datesElainein "The Abstinence". Ben and Elaine are having dinner when he informs her that he isn't actually a doctor yet, as he has taken doctor licensing exam three times but failed each time. Though Elaine is slightly relieved when she learns that she can still introduce Ben as "doctor."
